title: Marginal distributions
description: Practice calculating marginal distributions in two-way tables.

assessment_items.question_content:
James is interested in the relationship between weather conditions and whether the downtown train runs on time. For a year, James records the weather each day as well as whether this train arrives on time or is delayed. Here are his results:

Weather condition|On-time|Delayed | Total
- | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

**Find the marginal distribution of arrival status in counts.**

On-time: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

Delayed: [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: The marginal distribution of arrival status shows the totals for each arrival status, that is, how many total trains were on-time and how many were delayed.

The answer:

On-time: $330$

Delayed: $35$

assessment_items.question_content:
James is interested in the relationship between weather conditions and whether the downtown train runs on time. For a year, James records the weather each day as well as whether this train arrives on time or is delayed. Here are his results:

Weather condition|On-time|Delayed | Total
- | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

**Find the marginal distribution of weather condition in counts.**

Sunny: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

Cloudy: [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

Rainy: [[☃ numeric-input 3]]

Snowy: [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints: The marginal distribution of weather condition shows the totals for each weather condition, that is, how many total days were sunny, how many were cloudy, and so on.

The answer:

Sunny : $170$

Cloudy : $120$

Rainy : $55$

Snowy : $20$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here is a table giving the thousands of commuters in Alabama in $2015$ by transportation method and one-way duration of commute:

Commute duration (one-way) | Private vehicle | Public transportation | Other | Total
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
$<15$ minutes | $498$ | $1$ | $27$ | $526$
$15$-$29$ minutes | $748$ | $2$ | $8$ | $758$
$30$-$44$ minutes | $387$ | $2$ | $4$ | $393$
$45$-$59$ minutes | $145$ | $1$ | $0$ | $146$
$\geq60$ minutes | $109$ | $3$ | $3$ | $115$
Total | $1887$ | $9$ | $42$ | $1938$

[//]: (Derived from https://factfinder.census.gov/faces/tableservices/jsf/pages/productview.xhtml?pid=ACS_15_5YR_S0802&prodType=table)

**Find the marginal distribution of one-way duration of commute in counts.**

Less than $15$ minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$15$-$29$ minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

$30$-$44$ minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 3]]

$45$-$59$ minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 4]]

$60$ or more minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 6]]
hints: The marginal distribution of one-way duration of commute shows the totals for each duration, that is, how many total thousands of commuters had a commute less than $15$ minutes, how many had a commute between $15$ and $29$ minutes, and so on.

The answer:

Less than $15$ minutes: $526$

$15$-$29$ minutes: $758$

$30$-$44$ minutes: $393$

$45$-$59$ minutes: $146$

$60$ or more minutes: $115$

Public transportation: $9$

Other: $42$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here is a table giving the number of US households (in thousands) in $2013$ by tenure and insurance status:

Insurance status | Owns home | Rents home
-|-|-|-
Insured | $71$ | $13$ 
Uninsured | $5$ | $27$

[//]: (Source https://factfinder.census.gov/faces/tableservices/jsf/pages/productview.xhtml?pid=AHS_2013_C10AO&prodType=table)

**Find the marginal distribution of tenure in counts.**

Owns home: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

Rents home: [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: The marginal distribution of tenure shows the totals for each tenure, that is, how many total thousands of households own their homes and how many thousands of households rent.

Let's find the totals.

Owns home: $71+5=76$

Rents home: $13+27=40$

The answer:

Owns home: $76$

Rents home: $40$

answers.message: Good job noticing that the data is in thousands of households. Since the data is in thousands, give your answer in thousands, also.
Good job noticing that the data is in thousands of households. Since the data is in thousands, give your answer in thousands, also.

Here is a table giving the number of US households (in thousands) in $2013$ by tenure and insurance status:

|| Tenure ||
Insurance status | Owns home | Rents home 
-|-|-|-
Insured | $71$ | $13$ 
Uninsured | $5$ | $27$ 

[//]: (Source https://factfinder.census.gov/faces/tableservices/jsf/pages/productview.xhtml?pid=AHS_2013_C10AO&prodType=table)

**Find the marginal distribution of insurance status in counts.**

Insured: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

Uninsured: [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: The marginal distribution of insurance status shows the totals for each insurance status, that is, how many total thousands of households are insured and how many thousands are uninsured.

Let's find the totals.

Insured: $71+13=84$

Uninsured: $5+27=32$

The answer:

Insured: $84$

Uninsured: $32$

assessment_items.question_content:
Mavis is interested in whether military dependency is related to gender in American Samoa. Here is data she collected about the population of American Samoa in $2010$:

Military status | Female | Male 
-|-:|-:|-:
In Armed Forces | $28$ | $59$
Military-dependent | $928$ | $781$
Civilian | $26{,}393$ | $27{,}330$

[https://factfinder.census.gov/faces/tableservices/jsf/pages/productview.xhtml?pid=DEC_10_ASSF_PBG16&prodType=table]: (Source )

**Find the marginal distribution of gender in counts.**

Female: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

Male: [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: The marginal distribution of gender shows the totals for each gender, that is, how many total people are female and how many are male.

Let's find the totals.

Female: 

$\begin{align}
28\\
928\\
\underline{+26{,}393}\\
27{,}349
\end{align}$

Male: 

$\begin{align}
59\\
781\\
\underline{+27{,}330}\\
28{,}170
\end{align}$

The answer:

Female: $27{,}349$

Male: $28{,}170$

assessment_items.question_content:
Mavis is interested in whether military status is related to gender in American Samoa. Here is data she collected about the population of American Samoa in $2010$:

Military status | Female | Male
-|-:|-:|-:
In Armed Forces | $28$ | $59$
Military-dependent | $928$ | $781$
Civilian | $26{,}393$ | $27{,}330$ 

[https://factfinder.census.gov/faces/tableservices/jsf/pages/productview.xhtml?pid=DEC_10_ASSF_PBG16&prodType=table]: (Source )

**Find the marginal distribution of military status in counts.**

In Armed Forces: [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

Military-dependent: [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

Civilian: [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints: The marginal distribution of military status shows the totals for each military status, that is, how many people are in the Armed forces, how many are military-dependent, and so on.

Let's find the totals.

In Armed Forces: $28+59=87$

Military-dependent: $928+781=1709$ 

Civilian: $26{,}393+27{,}330=53{,}723$

The answer:

In Armed Forces: $87$

Military-dependent: $1709$ 

Civilian: $53{,}723$

assessment_items.question_content:
James is interested in the relationship between weather conditions and whether the downtown train runs on time. For a year, James records the weather each day as well as whether this train arrives on time or is delayed. Here are his results:

Weather condition|On-time|Delayed | Total
- | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

**Find the marginal distribution of arrival status in percentages.**  
*Round to the nearest whole percent.*

On-time: [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\%$

Delayed: [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\%$
hints: The marginal distribution of arrival status in percentages shows what percent of total trains were on-time and what percent were delayed.

On-time: $330/365\approx90\%$

Delayed: $35/365\approx10\%$

The answer:

On-time: $90\%$

Delayed: $10\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
James is interested in the relationship between weather conditions and whether the downtown train runs on time. For a year, James records the weather each day as well as whether this train arrives on time or is delayed. Here are his results:

Weather condition|On-time|Delayed | Total
- | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

**Find the marginal distribution of weather condition in percentages.**  
*Round to the nearest whole percent.*

Sunny: [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\%$

Cloudy: [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\%$

Rainy: [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\%$

Snowy: [[☃ numeric-input 4]] $\%$
hints: The marginal distribution of weather condition in percentages shows what percent of the total days were sunny, what percent were cloudy, and so on.

Sunny: $170/365\approx 47\%$

Cloudy: $120/365\approx 33\%$

Rainy: $55/365\approx 15\%$

Snowy: $20/365\approx 5\%$

The answer:

Sunny : $47\%$

Cloudy : $33\%$

Rainy : $15\%$

Snowy : $ 5\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here is a table giving the thousands of commuters in Alabama in $2015$ by transportation method and one-way duration of commute:

Commute duration (one-way) | Private vehicle | Public transportation | Other | Total
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
$<15$ minutes | $498$ | $1$ | $27$ | $526$
$15$-$29$ minutes | $748$ | $2$ | $8$ | $758$
$30$-$44$ minutes | $387$ | $2$ | $4$ | $393$
$45$-$59$ minutes | $145$ | $1$ | $0$ | $146$
$\geq60$ minutes | $109$ | $3$ | $3$ | $115$
Total | $1887$ | $9$ | $42$ | $1938$


[//]: (Derived from https://factfinder.census.gov/faces/tableservices/jsf/pages/productview.xhtml?pid=ACS_15_5YR_S0802&prodType=table)

**Find the marginal distribution of one-way duration of commute in percentages.**  
*Round to the nearest whole percent.*

Less than $15$ minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\%$

$15$-$29$ minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\%$

$30$-$44$ minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\%$

$45$-$59$ minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 4]] $\%$

$60$ or more minutes: [[☃ numeric-input 6]] $\%$
hints: The marginal distribution of one-way duration of commute shows what percent of commuters had a commute less than $15$ minutes, what percent had a commute between $15$ and $29$ minutes, and so on.

Less than $15$ minutes: $526 \div 1938 \approx 27\%$

$15$-$29$ minutes: $758 \div 1938 \approx 39\%$

$30$-$44$ minutes: $393 \div 1938 \approx 20\%$

$45$-$59$ minutes: $146 \div 1938 \approx 8\%$

$60$ or more minutes: $115 \div 1938 \approx 6\%$

The answer:

Less than $15$ minutes: $27\%$

$15$-$29$ minutes: $39\%$

$30$-$44$ minutes: $20\%$

$45$-$59$ minutes: $8\%$

$60$ or more minutes: $6\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here is a table giving the thousands of commuters in Alabama in $2015$ by transportation method and one-way length of commute:

Commute duration (one-way) | Private vehicle | Public transportation | Other | Total
- | -: | -: | -: | -:
$<15$ minutes | $498$ | $1$ | $27$ | $526$
$15$-$29$ minutes | $748$ | $2$ | $8$ | $758$
$30$-$44$ minutes | $387$ | $2$ | $4$ | $393$
$45$-$59$ minutes | $145$ | $1$ | $0$ | $146$
$\geq60$ minutes | $109$ | $3$ | $3$ | $115$
Total | $1887$ | $9$ | $42$ | $1938$


[//]: (Derived from https://factfinder.census.gov/faces/tableservices/jsf/pages/productview.xhtml?pid=ACS_15_5YR_S0802&prodType=table)

**Find the relative frequency marginal distribution of transportation method.**  
*Round to the nearest whole percent.*

Private vehicle: [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\%$

Public transportation: [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\%$

Other: [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\%$
hints: The relative frequency marginal distribution of transportation method shows what percent of commuters used private vehicles, what percent used public transportation, and so on.

Private vehicle: $1887 \div 1938 \approx 97\%$

Public transportation: $9 \div 1938 \approx 0\%$

Other: $42 \div 1938 \approx 2\%$

The answer:

Private vehicle: $97\%$

Public transportation: $0\%$

Other: $2\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
Here is a table classifying $116$ thousand  US households (in thousands) in $2013$ by tenure and insurance status:

Insurance status | Owns home | Rents home
-|-|-|-
Insured | $71$ | $13$ 
Uninsured | $5$ | $27$

[//]: (Source https://factfinder.census.gov/faces/tableservices/jsf/pages/productview.xhtml?pid=AHS_2013_C10AO&prodType=table)

**Find the marginal distribution of tenure in percentages.**  
*Round to the nearest whole percent.*

Owns home: [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\%$

Rents home: [[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\%$
hints: The marginal distribution of tenure shows the percentage for each tenure, that is, what percent of households own their homes and what percent of households rent their home.

Let's find the percentages.

Owns home: $(71+5) \div 116\approx 66\%$

Rents home: $(13+27) \div 116\approx 34\%$

The answer:

Owns home: $66\%$

Rents home: $34\%$

-|-:|-:|-:
In Armed Forces | $28$ | $59$ | $87$
Military-dependent | $928$ | $781$ | $1709$
Civilian | $26{,}393$ | $27{,}330$ | $53{,}723$
Total | $27{,}349$ | $28{,}170$ | $55{,}519$
